prior_direct_manifest.title: The Mirage of China’s Military Edge
prior_direct_manifest.body_preview: Skip to content Current Issue Archive Books Podcast Foreign Affairs Newsletters Log In Subscribe Menu New Issue Out July/August 2026 Read Now The Mirage of China’s Military Edge Panic Is Misguided—and Counterproductive Dennis Blair July/August 2026 Published on June 23, 2026 Matt Murphy Listen Share & Download Print Save If China were to seize control of Taiwan by force, it would be a disaster, not only for Taiwan but also for the United States. A nearly $1 trillion economy would leave the free-market system and be incorporated into China’s state-directed, mercantilist one. A vibrant democracy nurtured and defended by the United States for many years would be snuffed out. American power and influence would be gravely diminished in East Asia, and China would become the region’s dominant power. Other governments there would be pressured to accommodate China’s political, economic, and even territorial demands. Beijing would certainly insist that they kick out U.S. forces. China’s global a
